what_it_does: Reads a curated list of Markdown design documents from a scan file, sends each file's contents to a local chat-completion endpoint with a strict system prompt, and appends structured JSON extractions to a JSONL output file, resuming and deduplicating incrementally.
observations: Hardcoded ROOT path points to a Windows/WSL Documents directory and OUT points to ~/wizard-run-1/target_extract.jsonl.
Uses a local HTTP URL http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions and model cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit.

The system prompt asks the model to reconstruct a 'UNIVERSAL AGENT-DRIVEN INTERFACE SYSTEM' and return strict JSON with fields like system_role, purpose, mechanisms, contracts, UI, product_requirements, and unresolved items.
call(p,g) optionally sets response_format to json_object when g is true.
ex(path) reads up to 110,000 characters, skips files shorter than 40 characters, retries up to 4 times, and returns a record keyed by relative path.
Target files are selected from scan.jsonl by kind or keyword, deduplicated by basename, and filtered against already-processed entries in the output file.
Processing runs in parallel with an 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor and prints progress every 40 files.
standouts: The system prompt imposes a very specific product-oriented extraction schema tied to a 'wizard-of-wizards' universal agent-interface system.
It builds an incremental, resumable extraction pipeline over a pre-scanned document set.
It uses a local LLM endpoint rather than a managed API.
conventions: Global constants for paths, URL, model, and prompt.
Functional style with small helper functions and a main loop.
JSONL as both input scan and output format.
Retry loop with fixed sleeps on HTTP or parse errors.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute paths make the script environment-specific and fragile.
Deduplication by basename may discard distinct documents that share a filename in different folders.
No schema validation of the returned JSON beyond json.loads.
No handling for a missing scan.jsonl file.
max_tokens=1600 may truncate long design documents even though 110k characters are read.
No rate limiting or back-off beyond fixed 2-second sleeps.
notes: A local-LLM batch extraction harness that turns selected design Markdown files into structured product metadata records.
questions: What is the expected schema and content of scan.jsonl?
Is the model name cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit real/available in this environment?
How are the selected keywords and kinds calibrated, and do they capture all relevant design docs?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the system prompt is to extract structured descriptions of design documents for a universal agent-driven interface system; the script does exactly that by calling a local LLM, so purpose and behavior align.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to automatically catalog scattered AI-generated design documents into a consistent, queryable ledger of system intent, mechanisms, contracts, and gaps for a universal agent-driven UI platform.
novelty: The extraction is framed as reconstructing a single 'wizard-of-wizards' product vision, forcing every file through a product-requirements lens rather than plain summarization.
contribution: A resumable, parallel batch harness that transforms raw Markdown design artifacts into normalized JSONL records describing each file's role, mechanisms, data models, UI model, implied product requirements, and missing pieces.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that selects target Markdown design documents from a scan file and uses a local chat-completion LLM to produce structured JSONL extractions describing each file's contribution to a universal agent-driven interface system.
intention_for_embedding: To bootstrap a structured ledger of design intent by automatically interpreting and normalizing scattered AI-written design documents into a consistent product-oriented schema.

symbols.does: Sends a chat-completion request to a local HTTP endpoint with the system prompt, optional JSON response format, and retries, returning the parsed response content.
symbols.description: call(p, g) builds a JSON payload using global MODEL, SYS, URL, and timeout settings, posts it to http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions, and returns the parsed message content. When g is true it requests a JSON object response format. It does not implement its own retry logic; retries are handled by callers.

symbols.does: Reads a target file and invokes call repeatedly to extract structured metadata, returning a record keyed by relative path or an error descriptor.
symbols.description: ex(path) computes the relative path, reads up to 110,000 characters with loose encoding handling, skips files shorter than 40 characters, then calls the local LLM up to four times with a JSON-object request on the first two attempts. It returns the parsed extraction augmented with the relative path, or a short error record if reading or all retries fail.
